释义: ==抛弃，遗弃；放弃==
英文释义: to leave somebody, especially somebody you are responsible for; to stop doing an activity
词根词缀: a(加强)+band(带子)+on→带子被丢弃→抛弃
搭配: abandon hope, abandon the idea, abandon a plan
例句:
- Those who abandon hope will never achieve their goals.
- The crew abandoned the sinking ship.
释义_raw: 抛弃，遗弃；放弃

释义: ==n. 腹部\n[医] 腹[部]==
英文释义: n. the region of the body of a vertebrate between the thorax and the pelvis
词根词缀: ab-(away from)+domen(from Latin 'domus' meaning 'house')→literally 'away from the house (i.e., the thoracic 'home' of vital organs)'→refers to the cavity below the diaphragm, housing digestive organs
搭配: abdominal muscles, abdomen pain, upper abdomen
例句:
- The surgeon made an incision in the abdomen to access the appendix.
- She felt a sharp pain in her lower abdomen after the workout.
释义_raw: n. 腹部\n[医] 腹[部]

释义: ==vi. 停留, 遵守, 居留, 继续下去\nvt. 忍受, 经受, 屈从于==
英文释义: v dwell\nv put up with something or somebody unpleasant
词根词缀: ab-(away, from)+ide (Old English 'īdan', to remain, dwell)→literally 'to remain away (from transgression)' or 'to stay in place'→vi. to remain, dwell, or comply; vt. to endure (as in 'bearing up under' something)
搭配: abide by a rule/law/agreement, abide the consequences, abide in faith
例句:
- I cannot abide dishonesty in the workplace.
- She abides by the rules without question.
释义_raw: vi. 停留, 遵守, 居留, 继续下去\nvt. 忍受, 经受, 屈从于

释义: ==n. 能力, 才干\n[经] 能力, 才能==
英文释义: n. the quality of being able to perform; a quality that permits or facilitates achievement or accomplishment\nn. possession of the qualities (especially mental qualities) required to do something or get something done
词根词缀: abil(able, capable)+-ity(noun-forming suffix indicating state or quality)→state of being able→the quality or power to do something→ability
搭配: ability to do something, lack of ability, develop one's abilities
例句:
- She has an exceptional ability to learn languages quickly.
- The job requires strong analytical abilities and attention to detail.
释义_raw: n. 能力, 才干\n[经] 能力, 才能

释义: ==a. 反常的, 不规则的, 变态的, 畸形的\nn. 畸形的人==
英文释义: a. not normal; not typical or usual or regular or conforming to a norm\na. departing from the normal in e.g. intelligence and development\ns. much greater than the normal
搭配: abnormal growth, abnormal behavior, abnormal development
例句:
- Her blood pressure readings were consistently abnormal, prompting further medical investigation.
- The software displayed abnormal behavior after the latest update, crashing repeatedly without error messages.
释义_raw: a. 反常的, 不规则的, 变态的, 畸形的\nn. 畸形的人

释义: ==adv. 在船上, 在火车上, 在飞机上\nprep. 在...之上==
英文释义:
r. on a ship, train, plane or other vehicle
r. on first or second or third base
r. side by side
r. part of a group
同义词: on board, on the plane/train/ship, aboard the vessel
词根词缀: a-(on, in)+board(n. 甲板，船面)→字面义‘在甲板上’→引申为‘在交通工具之上’→词义：在船上/火车上/飞机上；在…之上
搭配: aboard the ship, aboard a flight, aboard the International Space Station
例句:
- All passengers must be aboard the aircraft 30 minutes before departure.
- She jumped aboard the moving train just as the doors were closing.
释义_raw: adv. 在船上, 在火车上, 在飞机上\nprep. 在...之上

释义: ==vt. 废止, 革除, 消灭\n[经] 废除, 取消, 裁撤==
英文释义: v. do away with
搭配: abolish slavery, abolish a law, abolish discrimination
例句:
- The government decided to abolish the outdated tax law.
- Many countries have abolished the death penalty in recent decades.
释义_raw: vt. 废止, 革除, 消灭\n[经] 废除, 取消, 裁撤

释义: ==vi. 大量存在, 富于, 充满==
英文释义: v. be abundant or plentiful; exist in large quantities\nv. be in a state of movement or action
词根词缀: a(在)+bound(边界)→在边界内→大量存在
搭配: abound in, abound with, abound everywhere
例句:
- Wildlife abounds in this national park.
- The library abounds with rare manuscripts.
释义_raw: vi. 大量存在, 富于, 充满

释义: ==adv. 往国外, 到室外, 到处\na. 往国外的, 在室外的, 广泛四散的==
英文释义: s. in a foreign country\nr. to or in a foreign country
词根词缀: a(在)+broad(宽)→在宽广的地方→国外
搭配: study abroad, live abroad, work abroad
例句:
- She studied abroad in Japan for a year.
- Many young people dream of working abroad after graduation.
释义_raw: adv. 往国外, 到室外, 到处\na. 往国外的, 在室外的, 广泛四散的

释义: ==a. 突然的, 唐突的, 陡峭的, 不连贯的==
英文释义:
s. marked by sudden changes in subject and sharp transitions
s. exceedingly sudden and unexpected
s. extremely steep
s. surprisingly and unceremoniously brusque in manner
词根词缀: ab(离开)+rupt(断裂)→突然断裂→突然的
搭配: abrupt change, abrupt stop, abrupt departure
例句:
- The meeting ended in an abrupt silence when the CEO walked in.
- The road makes an abrupt turn just before the cliff edge.
释义_raw: a. 突然的, 唐突的, 陡峭的, 不连贯的

释义: ==n. 缺席, 缺乏, 没有\n[医] 失神==
英文释义:
n. the state of being absent
n. failure to be present
n. the time interval during which something or somebody is away
n. the occurrence of an abrupt, transient loss or impairment of consciousness (which is not subsequently remembered), sometimes with light twitching, fluttering eyelids, etc.; common in petit mal epilepsy
搭配: absence from, in the absence of
例句:
- His absence from school was noticed.
- In the absence of evidence, we cannot proceed.
释义_raw: n. 缺席, 缺乏, 没有\n[医] 失神

释义: ==a. 缺席的, 不在的, 缺乏的, 漫不经心的\nvt. 使缺席==
英文释义: v. go away or leave\na. not being in a specified place\ns. lost in thought; showing preoccupation
词根词缀: ab-(away)+sent(from Latin 'esse', 'to be')→'not being (in a place)'→缺席的，不在的
搭配: absent from school, absent without leave (AWOL), absent-minded
例句:
- She was absent from work due to illness.
- He looked absent, as if his mind were elsewhere.
释义_raw: a. 缺席的, 不在的, 缺乏的, 漫不经心的\nvt. 使缺席

释义: ==a. 绝对的, 专制的, 完全的, 独立的\nn. 绝对事物==
英文释义:
n. something that is conceived or that exists independently and not in relation to other things; something that does not depend on anything else and is beyond human control; something that is not relative
a. perfect or complete or pure
s. complete and without restriction or qualification; sometimes used informally as intensifiers
s. not limited by law
词根词缀: ab(脱离)+solute(松开)→脱离限制的→绝对的
搭配: absolute zero, absolute power, absolute truth
例句:
- I have absolute confidence in you.
- The absolute truth is hard to find.
释义_raw: a. 绝对的, 专制的, 完全的, 独立的\nn. 绝对事物

释义: ==吸收；吸引…的注意；使全神贯注==
英文释义: to take in a liquid, gas or heat; to keep somebody's attention
搭配: be absorbed in, absorb attention, absorb information
例句:
- The book absorbs readers with its vivid descriptions.
- Plants absorb carbon dioxide from the atmosphere.
释义_raw: 吸收；吸引…的注意；使全神贯注

释义: ==抽象的；摘要，概论==
英文释义: existing as an idea rather than as something physical; a summary
搭配: abstract idea, abstract art, in the abstract
例句:
- Beauty is an abstract concept.
- The paper begins with an abstract.
释义_raw: 抽象的；摘要，概论

释义: ==a. 荒谬的, 不合理的, 可笑的\nn. 荒诞==
英文释义: n. a situation in which life seems irrational and meaningless\ns. inconsistent with reason or logic or common sense\ns. incongruous; inviting ridicule
词根词缀: ab(偏离)+surd(声音)→声音不协调→荒谬的
搭配: absurd idea, absurd situation, absurdly exaggerated
例句:
- It's absurd to think that the earth is flat in the 21st century.
- The play explores the absurd of modern bureaucracy through dark humor.
释义_raw: a. 荒谬的, 不合理的, 可笑的\nn. 荒诞

释义: ==a. 丰富的, 充分的, 大量的==
英文释义: a. present in great quantity
词根词缀: ab-(away, from)+und(=wave, flowing)+-ant(adj. suffix)→'flowing away' or 'overflowing'→ suggesting overflowing quantity → abundant
搭配: abundant evidence, abundant opportunity, abundant rainfall
例句:
- The region is abundant in natural resources such as oil and timber.
- She has an abundant supply of patience when teaching beginners.
释义_raw: a. 丰富的, 充分的, 大量的

释义: ==n. 滥用, 虐待, 恶习, 辱骂\nvt. 滥用, 辱骂, 虐待==
英文释义: n. a rude expression intended to offend or hurt\nv. use foul or abusive language towards\nv. use wrongly or improperly or excessively
词根词缀: ab(偏离)+use(使用)→不当使用→滥用
搭配: drug abuse, abuse of power, verbal abuse
例句:
- Drug abuse is a serious problem.
- He was accused of abusing his power.
释义_raw: n. 滥用, 虐待, 恶习, 辱骂\nvt. 滥用, 辱骂, 虐待

释义: ==a. 学院的, 学术的, 不切实际的\nn. 大学生, 大学教师, 学者, 学会会员==
英文释义: a. associated with academia or an academy\ns. hypothetical or theoretical and not expected to produce an immediate or practical result\ns. marked by a narrow focus on or display of learning especially its trivial aspects
搭配: academic achievement, academic year, academic integrity
例句:
- Her academic achievements earned her a full scholarship to Oxford.
- He has been an academic at Harvard for over twenty years, specializing in medieval history.
释义_raw: a. 学院的, 学术的, 不切实际的\nn. 大学生, 大学教师, 学者, 学会会员

释义: ==n. 学院, 院校, 学会==
英文释义:
n. a secondary school (usually private)
n. an institution for the advancement of art or science or literature
n. a school for special training
n. a learned establishment for the advancement of knowledge
词根词缀: akadēm- (from Akadēmeia, the garden near Athens where Plato taught) + -y (noun-forming suffix denoting 'place or institution') → a place of learning or scholarly association → 'institution for specialized education or research'
搭配: military academy, academy award, academy of sciences
例句:
- She enrolled in a prestigious dance academy in New York.
- The Royal Academy of Arts hosts annual exhibitions of contemporary British artists.
释义_raw: n. 学院, 院校, 学会

释义: ==v. 加速, 促进==
英文释义: v. move faster\nv. cause to move faster
搭配: accelerate growth, accelerate development, accelerate progress
例句:
- The car accelerated rapidly as it merged onto the highway.
- Investment in renewable energy is expected to accelerate the transition to a low-carbon economy.
释义_raw: v. 加速, 促进

释义: ==n. 重音, 口音, 特点, 注重点\nvt. 重读, 加重音号于, 强调==
英文释义: n. distinctive manner of oral expression\nn. a diacritical mark used to indicate stress or placed above a vowel to indicate a special pronunciation
词根词缀: ad-(to)+centum(100, via Latin 'cantus' meaning 'song' or 'singing')→ originally 'to sing along with' → evolved to mean 'to mark for prominence in speech' → n. prominent stress or distinctive pronunciation; vt. to emphasize or mark phonetically
搭配: foreign accent, regional accent, place emphasis on (synonymous usage in verb sense)
例句:
- Her French accent made her English sound especially elegant.
- The teacher asked students to accent the second syllable of 'photograph'.
释义_raw: n. 重音, 口音, 特点, 注重点\nvt. 重读, 加重音号于, 强调

释义: ==vt. 接受, 承认, 同意, 相信, 赞成, 承担, 承兑, 采纳, 接纳, 容忍\nvi. 同意==
英文释义:
v. consider or hold as true
v. receive willingly something given or offered
v. give an affirmative reply to; respond favorably to
v. react favorably to; consider right and proper
词根词缀: ac-(to, towards)+cept(take, seize)→'take towards oneself'→voluntarily take or receive something offered, acknowledged, or imposed
搭配: accept responsibility, accept an invitation, accept payment
例句:
- She accepted the job offer without hesitation.
- The committee refused to accept the flawed data as evidence.
释义_raw: vt. 接受, 承认, 同意, 相信, 赞成, 承担, 承兑, 采纳, 接纳, 容忍\nvi. 同意

释义: ==n. 接受, 接纳, 承认, 同意, 赞同, 容忍, 相信\n[经] 承兑, 认付, (工程)验收==
英文释义:
n. the state of being acceptable and accepted
n. (contract law) words signifying consent to the terms of an offer (thereby creating a contract)
n. banking: a time draft drawn on and accepted by a bank
n. the act of taking something that is offered
词根词缀: accept(接受)+-ance(名词后缀，表行为、状态)→表示‘接受’这一行为或所处的状态→接受, 接纳, 承认等抽象名词义
搭配: acceptance rate, terms of acceptance, letter of acceptance
例句:
- Her acceptance of the job offer was announced at the team meeting.
- The project’s success depended on the client’s formal acceptance of the final deliverables.
释义_raw: n. 接受, 接纳, 承认, 同意, 赞同, 容忍, 相信\n[经] 承兑, 认付, (工程)验收

释义: ==n. 通路, 入口, 接近, 进入, 使用权, 发作\nvt. 访问, 存取, 接近, 使用\n[计] 访问, 存取==
英文释义:
n. the right to obtain or make use of or take advantage of something (as services or membership)
n. a way of entering or leaving
n. a code (a series of characters or digits) that must be entered in some way (typed or dialed or spoken) to get the use of something (a telephone line or a computer or a local area network etc.)
n. (computer science) the operation of reading or writing stored information
搭配: access to, have access, gain access
例句:
- Students need access to good books.
- Do you have access to the internet?
释义_raw: n. 通路, 入口, 接近, 进入, 使用权, 发作\nvt. 访问, 存取, 接近, 使用\n[计] 访问, 存取

释义: ==n. 附件, 零件, 附加物, 从犯, 同谋\na. 附属的, 补充的, 同谋的, 帮凶的, 非主要的, 副的\n[计] 附件==
英文释义:
n. clothing that is worn or carried, but not part of your main clothing
n. a supplementary component that improves capability
n. someone who helps another person commit a crime
s. furnishing added support
词根词缀: access(接近，进入)+-ory(表示‘与…有关的’形容词后缀)→与‘进入/获取’相关的事物→引申为‘辅助性物品’或‘协助犯罪的人’→词义：附件；从犯
搭配: fashion accessory, car accessory, accessory to crime
例句:
- She wore a silk scarf as a fashionable accessory.
- He was charged as an accessory to the robbery for driving the getaway car.
释义_raw: n. 附件, 零件, 附加物, 从犯, 同谋\na. 附属的, 补充的, 同谋的, 帮凶的, 非主要的, 副的\n[计] 附件

释义: ==n. 意外事件, 机遇, 事故, 次要方面\n[化] 意外事故; 事故==
英文释义: n. an unfortunate mishap; especially one causing damage or injury\nn. anything that happens suddenly or by chance without an apparent cause
词根词缀: ac(加强)+cid(落下)+ent→意外落下→事故
例句:
- He was injured in a car accident.
- It was no accident that they met.
释义_raw: n. 意外事件, 机遇, 事故, 次要方面\n[化] 意外事故; 事故

释义: ==n. 喝彩, 欢呼, 赞同\nv. 欢呼, 喝彩, 称赞==
英文释义: n. enthusiastic approval\nv. praise vociferously
词根词缀: ad-(to, toward)+clamare(to cry out)→'cry out toward'→ publicly express enthusiastic approval→ 'acclaim'
搭配: receive acclaim, acclaim as, acclaim for
例句:
- The film received widespread acclaim from critics around the world.
- She was acclaimed as a visionary leader at the international summit.
释义_raw: n. 喝彩, 欢呼, 赞同\nv. 欢呼, 喝彩, 称赞

释义: ==vt. 使适应, 调和, 通融, 容纳, 向...提供\nvi. 适应==
英文释义: v. provide with something desired or needed\nv. have room for; hold without crowding\nv. make (one thing) compatible with (another)
词根词缀: ac(加强)+com(共同)+mod(模式)+ate→使适应模式→容纳
搭配: accommodate someone's needs, accommodate a request, accommodate changes
例句:
- The hotel can accommodate up to 300 guests.
- She had to accommodate her schedule to her child's school hours.
释义_raw: vt. 使适应, 调和, 通融, 容纳, 向...提供\nvi. 适应

释义: ==n. 膳宿, 预订铺位, 适应性调节, 调和, 贷款\n[医] 调节(眼); 适应==
英文释义:
n. a settlement of differences
n. in the theories of Jean Piaget: the modification of internal representations in order to accommodate a changing knowledge of reality
n. living quarters provided for public convenience
n. the act of providing something (lodging or seat or food) to meet a need
词根词缀: ac-(to)+commod-(fit, suit)+-ation(noun suffix)→'to fit or suit together'→a process or result of fitting, adapting, or providing suitable living/functional conditions
搭配: make accommodation for, accommodation booking, on-campus accommodation
例句:
- The hotel offers excellent accommodation for international visitors.
- Her eyes' accommodation to low light took several minutes after leaving the bright room.
释义_raw: n. 膳宿, 预订铺位, 适应性调节, 调和, 贷款\n[医] 调节(眼); 适应

释义: ==vt. 陪伴, 伴随, 补充, 为...伴奏\nvi. 伴奏, 伴唱==
英文释义: v. go or travel along with
搭配: accompany someone to, accompany with music, accompany a meal
例句:
- She will accompany her friend to the hospital tomorrow.
- The violinist accompanied the singer on the piano.
释义_raw: vt. 陪伴, 伴随, 补充, 为...伴奏\nvi. 伴奏, 伴唱

释义: ==完成，实现，达到==
英文释义: to succeed in doing or completing something
搭配: accomplish a task, accomplish goals, accomplish a mission
例句:
- She accomplished her mission successfully.
- We have accomplished all we set out to do.
释义_raw: 完成，实现，达到

释义: ==n. 一致, 调和, 协定\nvt. 给与, 使一致\nvi. 相符合==
英文释义: n. concurrence of opinion\nn. sympathetic compatibility\nv. allow to have
搭配: in accord with, of one's own accord
例句:
- The two parties reached an accord after weeks of negotiation.
- Her actions do not accord with her stated principles.
释义_raw: n. 一致, 调和, 协定\nvt. 给与, 使一致\nvi. 相符合

释义: ==n. 一致, 和谐==
英文释义: n. the act of granting rights
词根词缀: ac-(to)+cord-(heart, agreement)+-ance(noun suffix)→'to heart' → mutual alignment or agreement → consistency/harmony
搭配: in accordance with
例句:
- The policy was implemented in accordance with national regulations.
- Her actions were not in accordance with the company's ethical guidelines.
释义_raw: n. 一致, 和谐

释义: ==根据, 按照；取决于；据…所说==
英文释义: as stated by or based on the information provided by someone or something
同义词: in accordance with, based on, as stated by
词根词缀: ac-(to, toward)+cord-(heart, agreement)+ing(gerund suffix)+to(preposition)→literally 'in heartward alignment' → expressing conformity or dependence on a source or standard→'according to'
搭配: according to plan, according to schedule, according to research
例句:
- According to the latest research, regular physical activity significantly reduces the risk of cardiovascular disease.
- The essay's conclusion must be revised according to the professor's feedback.
释义_raw: 根据, 按照；取决于；据…所说

释义: ==adv. 相应地, 因此, 于是==
英文释义: r. in accordance with
词根词缀: ac-(to)+cord(heart, agreement)+-ing(gerund/adverbial suffix)+-ly(adverb-forming suffix)→'in agreement (with what precedes)'→'in a way that matches or follows logically from what has been stated'→'accordingly'
搭配: accordingly to plan, accordingly to evidence, act accordingly
例句:
- The weather was extremely cold; accordingly, the school canceled outdoor activities.
- She had no formal training in coding, and accordingly, she started with beginner-level courses.
释义_raw: adv. 相应地, 因此, 于是

释义:
==n. 报告, 解释, 估价, 理由, 利润, 算账, 帐目
vi. 报帐, 解释, 导致, 报偿, 占, 杀死
vt. 认为
[计] 帐户, 帐号==
英文释义:
n. a formal contractual relationship established to provide for regular banking or brokerage or business services
n. importance or value
n. a statement of recent transactions and the resulting balance
n. the quality of taking advantage
搭配: account for, take into account, on account of
例句:
- She gave a detailed account of the incident to the police.
- His actions account for the company's recent success.
释义_raw:
n. 报告, 解释, 估价, 理由, 利润, 算账, 帐目
vi. 报帐, 解释, 导致, 报偿, 占, 杀死
vt. 认为
[计] 帐户, 帐号

释义: ==n. 会计人员, 会计师\n[经] 会计师, 会计人员==
英文释义: n. someone who maintains and audits business accounts

释义: ==n. 疼痛\nvi. 痛, 哀怜, 渴望==
英文释义: n. a dull persistent (usually moderately intense) pain\nv. have a desire for something or someone who is not present\nv. be the source of pain
词根词缀: Proto-Germanic *akiz (pain, sorrow) → Old English āce → no productive modern affix; the word is root-only, reflecting an ancient Indo-European root *ag- (to sharpen, be painful) → semantic shift from 'sharp sensation' to generalized physical or emotional pain
搭配: headache, toothache, ache for something
例句:
- My head has been throbbing with a dull ache all morning.
- She ached for her family after moving overseas.
释义_raw: n. 疼痛\nvi. 痛, 哀怜, 渴望

英文释义: n. a hose that carries air under pressure\nn. a commercial enterprise that provides scheduled flights for passengers
词根词缀: air(空气，天空)+line(线路，航线)→字面指‘空中线路’，引申为运营空中运输服务的公司→航空公司
搭配: major airline, budget airline, airline ticket
例句:
- She works for a major international airline.
- The airline announced new direct flights to Tokyo next summer.
释义_raw: n. 航线, 航线的设备, 航空公司

释义: ==n. 飞机场\n[机] 航空站, 机场==
英文释义: n. an airfield equipped with control tower and hangars as well as accommodations for passengers and cargo
词根词缀: air(空气，航空)+port(港口，停泊处)→航空器停泊/起降的场所→飞机场
搭配: airport security, airport terminal, arrive at the airport

- We arrived at the airport two hours before our international flight.
- The new high-speed rail connects the city center directly to the airport.

释义: ==n. 走廊, 侧廊, 过道\n[机] 走道==
英文释义: n. a long narrow passage (as in a cave or woods)\nn. passageway between seating areas as in an auditorium or passenger vehicle or between areas of shelves of goods as in stores\nn. part of a church divided laterally from the nave proper by rows of pillars or columns
词根词缀: Old French 'ele' (wing of a church, lateral division)+influence of 'isle' (due to spelling folk etymology)→no true native English root/affix; modern form reflects orthographic assimilation rather than morphological derivation→n. a longitudinal passageway, especially between rows of seats or in a church
搭配: down the aisle, aisle seat, center aisle
例句:
- Please walk down the aisle to your seat.
- She walked down the aisle in a beautiful white gown.
释义_raw: n. 走廊, 侧廊, 过道\n[机] 走道

释义: ==n. 惊恐, 警报, 警钟\nvt. 使惊恐, 警告\n[计] 报警信号==
英文释义:
n. fear resulting from the awareness of danger
n. a device that signals the occurrence of some undesirable event
n. an automatic signal (usually a sound) warning of danger
v. warn or arouse to a sense of danger or call to a state of preparedness
搭配: raise an alarm, set off an alarm, alarm clock
例句:
- The fire alarm went off at midnight, waking everyone in the building.
- She was alarmed by the sudden drop in her computer's performance and ran a virus scan.

英文释义: conj. Even though; although; notwithstanding.
词根词缀: all(全部)+be(it)+it(古英语宾格代词，表‘此’或‘它’)→字面义‘尽管是全部（如此）’→引申为让步连词‘尽管，虽然’
搭配: albeit + adjective, albeit + adverb, albeit + noun phrase
例句:
- The plan was successful, albeit costly.
- She accepted the offer, albeit with some hesitation.

释义: ==n. 粘贴簿, 唱片套\n[医] 白色物==
英文释义: n. one or more recordings issued together; originally released on 12-inch phonograph records (usually with attractive record covers) and later on cassette audiotape and compact disc\nn. a book of blank pages with pockets or envelopes; for organizing photographs or stamp collections etc
词根词缀: albus(拉丁语，意为'白色')+ -um(中性名词后缀)→原指古罗马时期用白蜡涂覆的书写板（tabula alba），后引申为记录文字/图像的簿册→粘贴簿；因早期唱片套内衬常为白色纸板，且作为音乐作品集合载体，故引申为'唱片集'
搭配: photo album, music album, album cover
例句:
- She carefully pasted her vacation photos into a leather-bound album.
- The band's latest studio album debuted at number one on the Billboard chart.
释义_raw: n. 粘贴簿, 唱片套\n[医] 白色物

释义: ==n. 酒精, 酒\n[化] 醇; 乙醇; 酒精==
英文释义: n. a liquor or brew containing alcohol as the active agent\nn. any of a series of volatile hydroxyl compounds that are made from hydrocarbons by distillation
词根词缀: Arabic 'al-kuḥl'(fine powder, later distilled essence)+English lexicalization→'al-' (definite article) + 'kuḥl' (originally antimony powder used as eyeliner; later extended to any purified or distilled substance)→via alchemy to denote distilled spirits, then specifically ethanol
搭配: alcohol abuse, alcohol content, alcohol poisoning
例句:
- Excessive alcohol consumption can damage the liver.
- This hand sanitizer contains 70% alcohol by volume.
释义_raw: n. 酒精, 酒\n[化] 醇; 乙醇; 酒精

英文释义:
n. condition of heightened watchfulness or preparation for action
n. a warning serves to make you more alert to danger
a. engaged in or accustomed to close observation
s. quick and energetic
词根词缀: al(完全)+ert(警戒)→完全警戒→警觉的
搭配: on alert, alert system, alert someone to something
例句:
- The security guard remained alert throughout the night shift.
- She was alerted to the error by her colleague's email.
释义_raw: a. 警觉的, 灵敏的, 留心的\nn. 警报\nvt. 使警觉, 通知, 使意识到

释义: ==n. 算法\n[计] 算法==
英文释义: n. a precise rule (or set of rules) specifying how to solve some problem
词根词缀: al-Khwarizmi(人名，波斯数学家花拉子米)+-thm(名词后缀，表过程或系统方法)→源自对花拉子米著作的拉丁文译名Algoritmi de numero Indorum→指其系统化算术方法→词义：为解决特定问题而定义的一组明确、有限、可执行的步骤
搭配: sorting algorithm, search algorithm, algorithm design
例句:
- This sorting algorithm has a time complexity of O(n log n).
- Machine learning models rely on sophisticated algorithms to identify patterns in data.
释义_raw: n. 算法\n[计] 算法

英文释义: v. transfer property or ownership\ns. not contained in or deriving from the essential nature of something\ns. being or from or characteristic of another place or part of the world
词根词缀: ali(其他)+en→其他地方的→外国的
搭配: illegal alien, alien registration, alien culture

例句:
- His harsh criticism alienated many of his longtime supporters.

释义: ==vi. 排列, 排成一行, 结盟\nvt. 使结盟, 使成一行, 校正==
英文释义:

词根词缀: ad-(to, toward)+linea(Latin: 'line')→'to bring into line'→arrange in a line or in agreement; adjust for consistency or cooperation

v. make angry

释义: ==任意的，武断的；专断的==
英文释义: based on random choice rather than reason or system
搭配: arbitrary decision, arbitrary choice, arbitrary power
例句:
- The decision seemed completely arbitrary.
- Arbitrary rules have no logical basis.
释义_raw: 任意的，武断的；专断的

释义:
==n. 拱门, 拱形, 足弓
vt. 使成弓形
vi. 拱起, 成弓形
a. 主要的, 调皮的, 傲慢无礼的, 狡猾的==
英文释义: A curved structure that supports weight above an opening; or to bend into a curved shape.
例句:
- The ancient Roman aqueduct features a series of graceful stone arches.
- She arched her eyebrows skeptically when he claimed to have finished the thesis early.
释义_raw:
n. 拱门, 拱形, 足弓
vt. 使成弓形
vi. 拱起, 成弓形
a. 主要的, 调皮的, 傲慢无礼的, 狡猾的

释义: ==n. 考古学==
英文释义: The scientific study of human history and prehistory through the excavation of sites and analysis of artifacts and other physical remains.
例句:
- Archaeology has provided crucial evidence about the daily life of ancient civilizations.
- Her dissertation in archaeology focused on Bronze Age burial practices in the Aegean region.
释义_raw: n. 考古学

释义: ==n. 建筑师, 设计者, 缔造者==
英文释义: a person who designs buildings or other structures, or who plans and directs the development of a complex system or project
例句:
- As the chief architect of the company's digital transformation, she restructured the entire IT infrastructure.
- The ancient Roman architect Vitruvius emphasized harmony, proportion, and durability in building design.
释义_raw: n. 建筑师, 设计者, 缔造者

释义: ==n. 建筑学, 建筑式样\n[计] 体系结构==
英文释义: the art and science of designing and constructing buildings; also, the structural design of a system, especially in computing
例句:
- The Gothic architecture of Notre-Dame Cathedral reflects medieval religious ideals and engineering ingenuity.
- Modern software development increasingly emphasizes modular architecture to enhance scalability and maintainability.
释义_raw: n. 建筑学, 建筑式样\n[计] 体系结构

释义: ==vt. 把...存档\nn. 档案馆, 档案文件\n[计] 挡案库, 存档==
英文释义: to store data or documents for long-term preservation; a collection of historical records or digital files
例句:
- The library archives rare manuscripts to ensure their preservation for future research.
- Before deleting the old project files, make sure to archive them in the secure cloud repository.
释义_raw: vt. 把...存档\nn. 档案馆, 档案文件\n[计] 挡案库, 存档

释义: ==n. 区域, 面积, 范围, 空地\n[计] 区域==
英文释义: A part of a surface or space; a geographical or functional region; the extent of a two-dimensional surface measured in square units.
例句:
- The research focuses on a specific area of cognitive neuroscience.
- Urban planners are concerned with the efficient use of limited land area in densely populated cities.
释义_raw: n. 区域, 面积, 范围, 空地\n[计] 区域

释义: ==n. 角斗场, 舞台, 场地\n[医] 尿沉渣, 尿沙; 脑沙==
英文释义: A place or setting where important events, conflicts, or competitions occur; also used metaphorically for a field of activity or debate.
例句:
- The international summit became the political arena for resolving the regional crisis.
- In medical diagnostics, calcified particles in urine sediment—known as 'arena'—may indicate chronic kidney disease.
释义_raw: n. 角斗场, 舞台, 场地\n[医] 尿沉渣, 尿沙; 脑沙

释义: ==vi. 提出理由, 争论, 辩论\nvt. 主张, 辩论, 证明, 说服==
英文释义: v. present reasons and arguments\nv. have an argument about something\nv. give evidence of
例句:
- They argued about politics.
- He argued that the plan would fail.
释义_raw: vi. 提出理由, 争论, 辩论\nvt. 主张, 辩论, 证明, 说服

释义: ==n. 争论, 论证, 论据, 自变量\n[计] 参数==
英文释义: A reason or set of reasons given in support of an idea, action, or theory; also, a disagreement or debate.
例句:
- The author presents a compelling argument for reducing carbon emissions in the opening chapter.
- In programming, the function call 'sqrt(16)' passes 16 as an argument to the square root function.
释义_raw: n. 争论, 论证, 论据, 自变量\n[计] 参数

释义: ==vi. 站立, 出现, 起来==
英文释义: v. originate or come into being\nv. rise to one's feet\nv. result or issue
搭配: arise from
例句:
- Problems may arise during the project.
- A new opportunity has arisen.
释义_raw: vi. 站立, 出现, 起来

释义: ==n. 算术, 算术知识\n[机] 算术; 算术的==
英文释义: The branch of mathematics dealing with the properties and manipulation of numbers, especially addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division.
例句:
- Her strong foundation in arithmetic enabled her to excel in advanced quantitative reasoning tasks on the GRE.
- The algorithm relies on basic arithmetic operations, making it efficient even on low-power embedded systems.
释义_raw: n. 算术, 算术知识\n[机] 算术; 算术的

释义: ==n. 军队, 陆军==
英文释义: a large organized group of soldiers trained for war
例句:
- The government deployed a contingent of the army to maintain order during the crisis.
- Historians argue that the decline of the Roman Empire was accelerated by the increasing reliance on mercenary troops rather than a loyal, professional army.
释义_raw: n. 军队, 陆军

释义: ==vt. 唤醒, 鼓励, 引起\nvi. 醒来==
英文释义: to cause a feeling, reaction, or interest to begin; to wake someone up
例句:
- The documentary aroused widespread public concern about climate change.
- His passionate speech aroused enthusiasm among the graduate students.
释义_raw: vt. 唤醒, 鼓励, 引起\nvi. 醒来

释义: ==v. 安排, 排列, 达成协议\n[计] 重排==
英文释义: to plan or organize something in advance; to put things in a particular order; to reach an agreement
例句:
- The committee arranged a series of lectures on climate change for graduate students.
- Both parties arranged to meet next Monday to finalize the terms of the research collaboration.
释义_raw: v. 安排, 排列, 达成协议\n[计] 重排

释义: ==n. 排列, 衣服, 大批, 军队\nvt. 布署, 打扮, 排列\n[计] 数组; 阵列==
英文释义: An ordered arrangement of elements, especially in rows and columns; also, a large and impressive group or collection.
例句:
- The data was stored in a two-dimensional array for efficient matrix operations.
- She entered the ballroom in a dazzling array of jewels and silk, commanding immediate attention.
释义_raw: n. 排列, 衣服, 大批, 军队\nvt. 布署, 打扮, 排列\n[计] 数组; 阵列

释义: ==n. 逮捕, 监禁\nvt. 拘捕, 抑制, 吸引, 阻止==
英文释义: the act of taking someone into custody by legal authority; to seize or stop something abruptly
例句:
- The police made an arrest after gathering sufficient evidence against the suspect.
- Her sudden illness arrested the progress of the clinical trial.
释义_raw: n. 逮捕, 监禁\nvt. 拘捕, 抑制, 吸引, 阻止

释义: ==n. 到达, 抵达, 到达者\n[经] 到达, 到达物==
英文释义: the act of reaching a destination or the time when someone or something arrives
例句:
- The arrival of the first snowfall marked the beginning of winter.
- Her timely arrival at the conference ensured that the keynote speech started as scheduled.
释义_raw: n. 到达, 抵达, 到达者\n[经] 到达, 到达物

释义: ==vi. 到达, 抵达==
英文释义: to reach a place, especially at the end of a journey
例句:
- After a 12-hour flight, we finally arrived in Tokyo exhausted but exhilarated.
- The committee will not make a decision until all stakeholders arrive and present their evidence.
释义_raw: vi. 到达, 抵达

释义: ==a. 自大的, 傲慢的==
英文释义: having or revealing an exaggerated sense of one's own importance or abilities
词根词缀: ar(加强)+rog(要求)+ant→一再要求→傲慢的
例句:
- His arrogant dismissal of opposing views undermined his credibility in the academic debate.
- The candidate's arrogant tone during the interview alienated several members of the selection committee.
释义_raw: a. 自大的, 傲慢的

释义: ==n. 箭, 箭状物, 箭头记号==
英文释义: A pointed projectile shot from a bow, or any symbol or shape resembling it, especially an indicator showing direction.
例句:
- The archer released the arrow with perfect aim, hitting the bullseye on the target.
- In the flowchart, each arrow denotes a logical transition from one process step to the next.
释义_raw: n. 箭, 箭状物, 箭头记号

释义: ==n. 人工制品, 制造物, 人为现象, 膺象, 矫作物, 古代文物\n[医] 人为现象, 人工产物==
英文释义: An object made by humans, typically one of historical or cultural interest; in medicine, a false or misleading result caused by external factors.
例句:
- The museum's collection includes Bronze Age artefacts such as pottery, tools, and ceremonial weapons.
- The blurred region on the MRI scan was later identified as an imaging artefact rather than a pathological lesion.
释义_raw: n. 人工制品, 制造物, 人为现象, 膺象, 矫作物, 古代文物\n[医] 人为现象, 人工产物

释义: ==n. 动脉, 干道, 要道\n[医] 动脉==
英文释义: A blood vessel that carries oxygenated blood from the heart to the body tissues (except pulmonary artery); also used metaphorically for a major route or channel.
例句:
- The coronary artery supplies blood to the heart muscle, and its blockage can lead to a myocardial infarction.
- During rush hour, Nanjing Road functions as a vital artery of Shanghai's urban transportation system.
释义_raw: n. 动脉, 干道, 要道\n[医] 动脉

释义: ==n. 文章, 冠词, 物品, 物件, 条款, 契约\n[计] 信件==
英文释义: A written composition on a specific subject, a grammatical word used before a noun to indicate definiteness or indefiniteness, or a distinct item or clause in a document or agreement.
例句:
- The journal published a groundbreaking article on artificial intelligence ethics.
- In the treaty, Article 12 outlines the obligations of signatory states regarding data protection.
释义_raw: n. 文章, 冠词, 物品, 物件, 条款, 契约\n[计] 信件

释义: ==a. 发音清晰的, 口才好的\nvt. 明白地说, 以关节连接, 使成为系统的整体==
英文释义: able to speak clearly and express ideas fluently; to express something clearly and precisely in words
词根词缀: articul(关节)+ate→像关节一样灵活→清楚表达
例句:
- She articulated her argument with remarkable clarity and logical coherence.
- The candidate struggled to articulate a coherent policy on climate change during the debate.
释义_raw: a. 发音清晰的, 口才好的\nvt. 明白地说, 以关节连接, 使成为系统的整体

释义: ==a. 人造的, 假的, 非原地产的\n[医] 人工的, 人造的, 伟牟==
英文释义: made or produced by human beings rather than occurring naturally; not genuine or spontaneous
例句:
- The museum displayed an artificial heart used in early medical experiments.
- Her smile seemed artificial, lacking the warmth and sincerity expected in such a heartfelt moment.
释义_raw: a. 人造的, 假的, 非原地产的\n[医] 人工的, 人造的, 伟牟

释义: ==n. 艺术家, 画家==
英文释义: a person who creates art, especially paintings or drawings
例句:
- The museum featured works by both emerging and established artists.
- As a meticulous artist, she spent months perfecting the composition and color palette of her latest painting.
释义_raw: n. 艺术家, 画家

释义: ==a. 艺术的, 艺术家的, 富有艺术性的==
英文释义: having a natural ability or strong appreciation for art; characterized by artistic skill, sensitivity, or expression
例句:
- Her artistic vision transformed the ordinary gallery space into an immersive sensory experience.
- The film's artistic direction, including its lighting and composition, earned widespread critical acclaim.
释义_raw: a. 艺术的, 艺术家的, 富有艺术性的

释义: ==vi. 上升, 追溯, 登高\nvt. 攀登==
英文释义: to move upward or climb; to rise in status, rank, or importance
例句:
- The climbers began to ascend the steep north face of the mountain at dawn.
- Her rapid promotion allowed her to ascend to the position of CEO within five years.
释义_raw: vi. 上升, 追溯, 登高\nvt. 攀登

释义: ==vt. 确定, 查明\n[法] 查明, 弄清, 确定==
英文释义: to find out or establish something with certainty, especially through investigation or examination
例句:
- The committee was tasked with ascertaining the cause of the financial irregularities.
- Before submitting the research proposal, she spent two weeks ascertaining whether similar studies had already been published.
释义_raw: vt. 确定, 查明\n[法] 查明, 弄清, 确定

释义: ==把…归因于；把…归于==
英文释义: to say or think that something is caused by something else
词根词缀: a(加强)+scribe(写)→归到…名下→归因于
搭配: ascribe to, ascribe importance to, ascribe a cause
例句:
- He ascribed his success to hard work.
- The painting is ascribed to da Vinci.
释义_raw: 把…归因于；把…归于

释义: ==a. 惭愧的, 羞耻的==
英文释义: feeling guilty or embarrassed because of one's actions, characteristics, or associations
例句:
- She was deeply ashamed of having lied to her professor.
- He felt ashamed to admit that he hadn't read the assigned text.
释义_raw: a. 惭愧的, 羞耻的

释义: ==adv. 向岸, 在岸上==
英文释义: to or on the land, especially after being at sea
例句:
- The crew managed to swim ashore after the shipwreck.
- All cargo must be unloaded ashore before midnight for customs inspection.
释义_raw: adv. 向岸, 在岸上

释义: ==n. 小声说的话, 旁白\nadv. 在一边, 离开, 另外==
英文释义: a remark made quietly to oneself or to another person, not intended to be heard by others; or to one side, out of the main focus
例句:
- The actor delivered an aside to the audience, revealing the character's true thoughts.
- She put the controversial proposal aside for now, focusing instead on more urgent matters.
释义_raw: n. 小声说的话, 旁白\nadv. 在一边, 离开, 另外

释义: ==a. 睡着的, 长眠的, 麻木的\nadv. 熟睡地==
英文释义: in a state of sleep; unconscious and not aware of surroundings
例句:
- The baby fell asleep the moment her head touched the pillow.
- He was so exhausted that he fell asleep during the lecture, completely unaware of the professor's question.
释义_raw: a. 睡着的, 长眠的, 麻木的\nadv. 熟睡地

释义: ==n. 外观, 方面, 面貌, 方向\n[医] 方面, 局面; 外观==
英文释义: a particular part or feature of something
词根词缀: as(朝向)+spect(看)→朝某方向看→方面
例句:
- The environmental aspect of the policy has drawn widespread criticism.
- From a linguistic aspect, this construction is highly unusual in formal written English.
释义_raw: n. 外观, 方面, 面貌, 方向\n[医] 方面, 局面; 外观

英文释义: to have a strong desire or ambition to achieve something, especially something requiring effort or dedication
例句:
- Many young scholars aspire to publish in top-tier academic journals.
- She aspires to become a neurosurgeon, having devoted over a decade to rigorous medical training.

释义: ==vt. 暗杀, 行刺\n[法] 暗杀, 行刺, 中伤==
英文释义: to murder a prominent person, especially for political or religious reasons
例句:
- The dictator was assassinated by opposition militants in a carefully planned operation.
- Historians continue to debate whether the assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand directly triggered World War I.
释义_raw: vt. 暗杀, 行刺\n[法] 暗杀, 行刺, 中伤

释义: ==n. 攻击, 袭击\nvt. 袭击, 攻击\nvi. 发动攻击==
英文释义: a violent physical or verbal attack; to attack someone physically or verbally
例句:
- The defendant was charged with aggravated assault after seriously injuring the victim.
- Critics have launched a sustained intellectual assault on the theory's underlying assumptions.
释义_raw: n. 攻击, 袭击\nvt. 袭击, 攻击\nvi. 发动攻击

释义: ==vt. 集合, 收集, 装配\nvi. 集合\n[计] 汇编==
英文释义: to gather people or things together in one place; to put together the parts of something, especially a machine or structure
词根词缀: as(一起)+semble(类似)→聚集到一起→集合
例句:
- The committee will assemble tomorrow to discuss the new policy.
- Engineers must carefully assemble the components according to the technical specifications.
释义_raw: vt. 集合, 收集, 装配\nvi. 集合\n[计] 汇编

释义: ==n. 与会者, 集会, 装配, 组件\n[化] 装配==
英文释义: a group of people gathered for a common purpose, or the act of fitting parts together to make a whole
例句:
- The school assembly was addressed by the principal on the importance of academic integrity.
- The engineer identified the faulty component during the final stage of assembly.
释义_raw: n. 与会者, 集会, 装配, 组件\n[化] 装配

释义: ==断言，宣称；维护==
英文释义: to state a fact or belief confidently and forcefully
词根词缀: as(加强)+sert(连接)→一再连接→断言
搭配: assert authority, assert control, strongly assert
例句:
- He asserted that the project would succeed.
- She asserted her rights as a citizen.
释义_raw: 断言，宣称；维护

释义: ==评估，评价；估算==
英文释义: to evaluate or estimate the nature, ability or quality of something
搭配: assess the impact, assess the risk, assess performance
例句:
- Teachers need to assess student progress regularly.
- The damage was assessed at $2 million.
释义_raw: 评估，评价；估算

释义: ==n. 资产, 有益的东西==
英文释义: A resource with economic value that an individual, corporation, or country owns or controls with the expectation that it will provide future benefit.
词根词缀: ass(分配)+et→分配到的财物→资产
例句:
- Human capital is a critical intangible asset for knowledge-based economies.
- The company's most valuable asset is its proprietary algorithm, which gives it a sustainable competitive advantage.
释义_raw: n. 资产, 有益的东西

释义: ==vt. 分配, 指派, 赋值\n[计] 赋值==
英文释义: to give someone a task, responsibility, or role; to allocate a value to a variable in programming
词根词缀: as(朝向)+sign(标记)→标记给某人→分配
例句:
- The professor assigned each student a research topic for the term paper.
- In Python, you can assign the result of a function call to a variable using the equals sign.
释义_raw: vt. 分配, 指派, 赋值\n[计] 赋值

释义: ==n. 分配, 功课, 任务, 转让, 归因, 陈述\n[计] 赋值==
英文释义: A task or piece of work assigned to someone, especially as part of their studies or job; also, the act of assigning or transferring rights or property.
例句:
- The professor announced a challenging research assignment due next Friday.
- In programming, an assignment operation stores a value in a variable using the equals sign (=).
释义_raw: n. 分配, 功课, 任务, 转让, 归因, 陈述\n[计] 赋值

释义: ==vt. 使同化, 吸收, 使相拟, 把...比作\nvi. 被吸收, 被同化==
英文释义: to absorb and integrate information, ideas, or people into a larger group or system; to become part of a culture or society
例句:
- Immigrants often struggle to assimilate into the dominant culture while preserving their native traditions.
- The brain rapidly assimilates new vocabulary when learners engage in meaningful, context-rich reading.
释义_raw: vt. 使同化, 吸收, 使相拟, 把...比作\nvi. 被吸收, 被同化

释义: ==n. 帮助, 协助\nvt. 帮助, 促进\nvi. 协助, 参加==
英文释义: to help someone do something, especially in a subordinate or supportive role
词根词缀: as(加强)+sist(站立)→站在一旁帮助→协助
例句:
- The research assistant will assist the professor in collecting and analyzing data.
- Effective communication can significantly assist in resolving workplace conflicts.
释义_raw: n. 帮助, 协助\nvt. 帮助, 促进\nvi. 协助, 参加

释义: ==n. 协助, 援助\n[经] 援助, 帮助==
英文释义: help or support given to someone to accomplish a task
例句:
- The research project was completed with the assistance of several graduate students.
- Applicants who require financial assistance must submit supporting documents by the deadline.
释义_raw: n. 协助, 援助\n[经] 援助, 帮助

释义: ==n. 助手, 助理, 助教\na. 有帮助的, 辅助的, 助理的==
英文释义: a person who helps someone, especially in a professional or academic setting
例句:
- The professor's research assistant collected data and prepared the literature review.
- She was promoted to assistant manager after successfully leading the project team.
释义_raw: n. 助手, 助理, 助教\na. 有帮助的, 辅助的, 助理的

释义:
==n. 同伴, 伙伴, 关联的事物
vt. 使联合, 使发生联系
vi. 交往
[计] 关联==
英文释义: to connect or link something or someone with something else in one's mind; a person connected with another in work, activity, or purpose
词根词缀: as(一起)+soci(社会)+ate→在社会中一起→联系
搭配: associate with, be associated with
例句:
- Many people automatically associate the color red with danger or urgency.
- She has been an associate professor at the university for over five years, actively collaborating with senior faculty on AI ethics research.
释义_raw:
n. 同伴, 伙伴, 关联的事物
vt. 使联合, 使发生联系
vi. 交往
[计] 关联

释义: ==n. 协会\n[计] 关联==
英文释义: an organized group of people with a common purpose or interest
例句:
- The International Association of Lawyers promotes legal cooperation across borders.
- There is a strong association between regular physical exercise and reduced risk of cardiovascular disease.
释义_raw: n. 协会\n[计] 关联

释义: ==假设，假定；承担；呈现==
英文释义: to suppose something to be the case without proof; to take on responsibility
搭配: assume responsibility, assume control, wrongly assume
例句:
- I assume you have read the report.
- She assumed responsibility for the project.
释义_raw: 假设，假定；承担；呈现

释义: ==n. 假定, 自负, 担任, 假装\n[经] 假定, 承担==
英文释义: the act of taking something to be true without proof, or the thing that is taken for granted
例句:
- The argument rests on the questionable assumption that economic growth always leads to improved well-being.
- Her promotion was based not on merit but on the mistaken assumption that she would conform to traditional managerial norms.
释义_raw: n. 假定, 自负, 担任, 假装\n[经] 假定, 承担

释义: ==n. 保证, 把握, 信心, 保险\n[经] 保证, 担保, 保险==
英文释义: a confident belief that something is true or will happen; a formal promise or guarantee, especially in business or insurance contexts
例句:
- Her calm demeanor gave the investors assurance that the project would be completed on time.
- The bank required a letter of assurance from the guarantor before approving the loan.
释义_raw: n. 保证, 把握, 信心, 保险\n[经] 保证, 担保, 保险

释义: ==保证，确保；使确信==
英文释义: to tell somebody that something is definitely true; to make certain
搭配: assure quality, assure safety, assure somebody of
例句:
- I can assure you of the quality.
- The doctor assured her that she would recover.
释义_raw: 保证，确保；使确信

释义: ==vt. 使惊讶==
英文释义: to surprise someone very much, often in a way that is impressive or shocking
例句:
- Her profound knowledge of quantum physics astonished the panel of senior professors.
- The speed with which the startup scaled its operations astonished industry analysts.
释义_raw: vt. 使惊讶

释义: ==n. 太空旅行者, 宇航员\n[法] 美国太空人==
英文释义: A person trained to travel and work in space.
词根词缀: astro(星星)+naut(航海者)→星际航海者→宇航员
例句:
- The astronaut conducted a series of experiments aboard the International Space Station.
- Becoming an astronaut requires not only exceptional physical fitness but also advanced expertise in engineering and science.
释义_raw: n. 太空旅行者, 宇航员\n[法] 美国太空人

释义: ==n. 天文学==
英文释义: The scientific study of celestial objects, space, and the physical universe beyond Earth's atmosphere.
词根词缀: astro(星星)+nomy(规律)→星星的规律→天文学
例句:
- Astronomy has revealed that our solar system is just a tiny part of a vast, expanding universe.
- Students pursuing physics often take advanced courses in astronomy to deepen their understanding of cosmology and astrophysics.
释义_raw: n. 天文学

释义: ==n. 运动员, 运动选手\n[医] 运动员==
英文释义: a person who is trained or skilled in sports, especially one who competes professionally or at a high level
例句:
- Elite athletes undergo rigorous training to maintain peak physical condition.
- The study examined the long-term cardiovascular health of former college athletes.
释义_raw: n. 运动员, 运动选手\n[医] 运动员

释义: ==n. 大气, 空气, 气氛\n[医] 大气; 大气压==
英文释义: The layer of gases surrounding Earth; also, the prevailing mood or feeling in a place or situation.
例句:
- The greenhouse effect occurs when certain gases trap heat in the Earth's atmosphere.
- The conference hall was filled with an atmosphere of intense anticipation and scholarly enthusiasm.
释义_raw: n. 大气, 空气, 气氛\n[医] 大气; 大气压

释义: ==n. 原子, 核能, 微粒, 微量\n[计] 原子==
英文释义: The smallest unit of an element that retains its chemical properties; a basic building block of matter.
词根词缀: a(不)+tom(切割)→不能再切割→原子
例句:
- Scientists believe that all matter is composed of atoms and subatomic particles.
- The nuclear reactor harnesses energy released when atoms are split—a process known as nuclear fission.
释义_raw: n. 原子, 核能, 微粒, 微量\n[计] 原子

释义: ==vt. 附上, 使依附, 使附属, 使喜爱, 系, 缚\nvi. 附属, 归属, 联系在一起\n[计] 挂接服务器命令, 关联, 挂接, 附加==
英文释义: v. cause to be attached\nv. be attached; be in contact with\nv. become attached
搭配: attach to, attach importance to
例句:
- Attach the file to the email.
- She is attached to her children.
释义_raw: vt. 附上, 使依附, 使附属, 使喜爱, 系, 缚\nvi. 附属, 归属, 联系在一起\n[计] 挂接服务器命令, 关联, 挂接, 附加

释义: ==n. 攻击, 抨击\nvt. 攻击, 抨击, 动手干\nvi. 攻击==
英文释义: to act aggressively against someone or something, either physically, verbally, or in a strategic context
例句:
- The opposition launched a fierce verbal attack on the government's new economic policy.
- She decided to attack the complex problem systematically rather than tackling it haphazardly.
释义_raw: n. 攻击, 抨击\nvt. 攻击, 抨击, 动手干\nvi. 攻击

释义: ==达到，获得，实现==
英文释义: to succeed in achieving something after a lot of effort
搭配: attain a goal, attain success, attain a standard
例句:
- She attained the highest score in the exam.
- The country attained independence in 1960.
释义_raw: 达到，获得，实现

释义: ==n. 尝试, 企图\nvt. 尝试, 企图==
英文释义: n. earnest and conscientious activity intended to do or accomplish something
搭配: attempt to do, make an attempt
例句:
- She attempted to climb the mountain.
- His first attempt failed.
释义_raw: n. 尝试, 企图\nvt. 尝试, 企图

释义: ==vt. 参加, 照料, 伴随\nvi. 专心于, 照顾, 服侍, 出席==
英文释义:
v. be present at (meetings, church services, university), etc.
v. take charge of or deal with
v. to accompany as a circumstance or follow as a result
v. give heed (to)
搭配: attend to, attend a meeting
例句:
- She attends the same school.
- The nurse attended to the patient.
释义_raw: vt. 参加, 照料, 伴随\nvi. 专心于, 照顾, 服侍, 出席

释义: ==n. 随从, 侍者, 出席者\na. 侍候的, 伴随的, 出席的, 在场的==
英文释义: A person who serves, accompanies, or is present at an event; often in a supportive or auxiliary role.
例句:
- The flight attendant provided passengers with safety instructions before takeoff.
- As an attendant at the academic conference, she assisted speakers with their presentation materials and managed registration.
释义_raw: n. 随从, 侍者, 出席者\na. 侍候的, 伴随的, 出席的, 在场的

释义: ==n. 注意, 注意力\n[计] 引起注意信号==
英文释义: the act of focusing one's mind on something
搭配: pay attention to, draw attention to
例句:
- The professor emphasized that close attention to detail is essential for success in academic writing.
- Her groundbreaking research has attracted widespread attention from scholars across the globe.
释义_raw: n. 注意, 注意力\n[计] 引起注意信号

释义: ==n. 态度, 看法, 姿势\n[医] 体态, 姿势, 态度==
英文释义: a person's feelings, opinions, or way of thinking about something
搭配: attitude to/toward
例句:
- Her positive attitude toward challenges contributed significantly to the team's success.
- The study found that students' attitudes toward learning English were strongly influenced by their teachers' feedback.
释义_raw: n. 态度, 看法, 姿势\n[医] 体态, 姿势, 态度

释义: ==n. 代理人, 律师\n[经] 律师, 代理人==
英文释义: A lawyer licensed to represent clients in court and provide legal advice.
词根词缀: at(朝向)+torney(转)→受托转达的人→代理人
例句:
- The defendant hired a prominent attorney to handle the complex corporate litigation.
- Under U.S. law, every person has the right to an attorney if charged with a serious crime.
释义_raw: n. 代理人, 律师\n[经] 律师, 代理人

释义: ==vt. 吸引, 诱惑\nvi. 有吸引力==
英文释义: v. direct toward itself or oneself by means of some psychological power or physical attributes\nv. be attractive to\nv. exert a force on (a body) causing it to approach or prevent it from moving away
例句:
- The museum attracts millions of visitors.
- Her charm attracted everyone.
释义_raw: vt. 吸引, 诱惑\nvi. 有吸引力

释义: ==a. 吸引人的, 有魅力的\n[法] 有吸引力的, 有迷惑力的==
英文释义: pleasing or appealing to the senses or mind, especially in a way that causes interest or desire
例句:
- The company offered an attractive salary package to recruit top talent.
- She presented a well-structured and attractive argument that persuaded the committee.
释义_raw: a. 吸引人的, 有魅力的\n[法] 有吸引力的, 有迷惑力的

释义: ==把…归因于；属性，特征==
英文释义: to regard something as being caused by somebody/something; a quality or feature
词根词缀: at(加强)+tribute(给予)→给予原因→归因于
搭配: attribute to, key attribute, personal attributes
例句:
- She attributes her success to determination.
- Kindness is one of her best attributes.
释义_raw: 把…归因于；属性，特征

释义: ==n. 拍卖\nvt. 拍卖==
英文释义: a public sale where goods or property are sold to the highest bidder
词根词缀: auct(增加)+ion→价格不断增加→拍卖
例句:
- The rare manuscript was sold at auction for over $2 million.
- The government decided to auction off surplus land to raise revenue.
释义_raw: n. 拍卖\nvt. 拍卖

释义: ==n. 听众, 观众, 读者\n[法] 听讼, 观众, 听众==
英文释义: the group of people who watch a performance, read a book, or listen to a speech
例句:
- The speaker adjusted her tone to better engage the academic audience.
- This novel has gained a wide international audience due to its universal themes.
释义_raw: n. 听众, 观众, 读者\n[法] 听讼, 观众, 听众

释义: ==a. 音频的, 声音的\n[电] 声频==
英文释义: relating to sound or the sense of hearing, especially in electronic transmission or recording
例句:
- The lecture was recorded in high-definition audio to ensure clarity for online learners.
- This software supports both video and audio playback, but the audio track must be synchronized with the visual content.
释义_raw: a. 音频的, 声音的\n[电] 声频

释义:
==n. 审计, 查帐
vt. 查(帐), 旁听
vi. 查账
[计] 查帐; 审查; 检查==
英文释义: A formal examination of financial records or systems to ensure accuracy and compliance.
例句:
- The company underwent a rigorous financial audit before the merger.
- Graduate students may audit the advanced linguistics course without receiving credit.
释义_raw:
n. 审计, 查帐
vt. 查(帐), 旁听
vi. 查账
[计] 查帐; 审查; 检查

释义: ==n. 听众席, 礼堂\n[医] 大讲堂, 大会堂==
英文释义: a large room or hall where people gather to watch performances, lectures, or meetings
例句:
- The speaker's voice echoed clearly throughout the vast auditorium.
- Students filled the university auditorium for the annual medical ethics lecture.
释义_raw: n. 听众席, 礼堂\n[医] 大讲堂, 大会堂

释义: ==v. 增加, 增长\n[计] 增加; 增量; 添加==
英文释义: to make something larger or greater in size, amount, or degree
例句:
- The company plans to augment its workforce by hiring fifty new engineers.
- To augment her research findings, the scholar incorporated data from three additional case studies.
释义_raw: v. 增加, 增长\n[计] 增加; 增量; 添加

释义: ==a. 耳的, 听觉的, 气氛的, 气味的, 光环的\n[医] 耳的; 先兆的==
英文释义: relating to the ear or the sense of hearing
例句:
- Students with strong aural skills can easily distinguish subtle pronunciation differences in English.
- The exam includes an aural comprehension section that tests listening accuracy and speed.
释义_raw: a. 耳的, 听觉的, 气氛的, 气味的, 光环的\n[医] 耳的; 先兆的

释义: ==真实的，真正的；可靠的==
英文释义: true to its nature or character; genuine
搭配: authentic experience, authentic source, fully authentic
例句:
- The document is authentic.
- She has an authentic talent for music.
释义_raw: 真实的，真正的；可靠的

释义: ==n. 作家, 作家的著作, 创始人\n[法] 作者, 著作人, 本人==
英文释义: a person who writes a book, article, or other text; the creator or originator of an idea, plan, or action
例句:
- The author's meticulous research is evident throughout the monograph.
- She is widely regarded as the author of the modern theory of cognitive development.
释义_raw: n. 作家, 作家的著作, 创始人\n[法] 作者, 著作人, 本人

释义: ==n. 权力, 当权者, 当局, 权威, 专家\n[经] 代理权, 授权, 权威==
英文释义: The power or right to give orders, make decisions, and enforce obedience; a person or organization with official power or expertise in a particular field.
例句:
- The local authorities imposed strict regulations to control air pollution.
- As an authority on medieval literature, she was invited to keynote the international conference.
释义_raw: n. 权力, 当权者, 当局, 权威, 专家\n[经] 代理权, 授权, 权威

释义: ==n. 自动手枪, 自动机械\na. 自动的, 机械的, 必然的, 无意识的==
英文释义: operating independently of human control or conscious thought
词根词缀: auto(自己)+mat(动)+ic→自己动的→自动的
例句:
- The factory has adopted automatic assembly lines to improve efficiency.
- She reacted with automatic suspicion when asked about her past experience.
释义_raw: n. 自动手枪, 自动机械\na. 自动的, 机械的, 必然的, 无意识的

释义: ==n. 自动化, 自动操作\n[计] 自动学; 自动化==
英文释义: The use of machines, computers, or robots to do work that was previously done by people.
例句:
- The rapid advancement of automation has transformed manufacturing industries worldwide.
- Many economists warn that widespread automation may displace low-skilled workers and widen income inequality.
释义_raw: n. 自动化, 自动操作\n[计] 自动学; 自动化

释义: ==n. 汽车\n[机] 汽车==
英文释义: a road vehicle with four wheels, powered by an internal combustion engine or electric motor, used for transporting people
词根词缀: auto(自己)+mobile(移动的)→自动移动的车→汽车
例句:
- The rapid development of the automobile industry has significantly transformed urban planning and commuting patterns.
- In many developed countries, the widespread ownership of automobiles has contributed to both economic growth and environmental challenges.
释义_raw: n. 汽车\n[机] 汽车

释义: ==n. 自治, 自治权\n[医] 自主性==
英文释义: the right or ability to govern oneself or make independent decisions
词根词缀: auto(自己)+nomy(法律)→自己立法→自治
例句:
- The university has been granted greater autonomy in setting its own curriculum and hiring policies.
- Respecting patient autonomy is a fundamental principle in medical ethics, requiring informed consent before treatment.
释义_raw: n. 自治, 自治权\n[医] 自主性

释义: ==n. 辅助者, 辅助物, 附属机构, 助动词\na. 辅助的, 备用的==
英文释义: A person, thing, or part that provides help or support; in grammar, a verb (e.g., 'be', 'have', 'do') used with a main verb to form tenses, moods, or voices.
例句:
- The hospital opened an auxiliary clinic in the rural area to extend medical services.
- In the sentence 'She has finished her thesis', 'has' is an auxiliary verb forming the present perfect tense.
释义_raw: n. 辅助者, 辅助物, 附属机构, 助动词\na. 辅助的, 备用的

释义: ==利用；效用，利益==
英文释义: to help or be useful; benefit or advantage
搭配: avail oneself of, of no avail, to little avail
例句:
- He availed himself of the opportunity.
- All their efforts were of no avail.
释义_raw: 利用；效用，利益

释义: ==a. 可利用的, 可获得的, 有效的\n[医] 有效的, 可得的==
英文释义: able to be used, obtained, or accessed
搭配: available for, readily available
例句:
- The research data is not yet available to the public.
- Only candidates with relevant experience will be considered; qualified applicants are currently available in limited numbers.
释义_raw: a. 可利用的, 可获得的, 有效的\n[医] 有效的, 可得的

释义: ==n. 大街, 途径, 林荫路==
英文释义: a broad street, especially one lined with trees; a means or opportunity to achieve something
例句:
- The city's main avenue is lined with historic buildings and mature plane trees.
- Scientific research provides a vital avenue for addressing global climate challenges.
词根词缀: a(向)+venue(来)→人们来去的路→林荫道
释义_raw: n. 大街, 途径, 林荫路

释义:
==n. 平均, 平均数, 一般水平, 海损
a. 平均的, 中等的, 平常的
vt. 算出...平均数, 平均做, 均分, 使平衡
vi. 平均为, 呈中间色==
英文释义: a number representing the typical or central value in a set of data, calculated by summing values and dividing by the count; also used to describe something ordinary or intermediate in quality, quantity, or degree
例句:
- The average annual rainfall in this region is 1,200 mm, making it suitable for rice cultivation.
- While some students scored exceptionally high, the class average on the GRE quantitative section was 158, reflecting solid but not outstanding performance.
词根词缀: a(向)+ver(真实)+age(名词)→接近真实→平均
释义_raw:
n. 平均, 平均数, 一般水平, 海损
a. 平均的, 中等的, 平常的
vt. 算出...平均数, 平均做, 均分, 使平衡
vi. 平均为, 呈中间色

释义: ==vt. 转开, 避免, 防止==
英文释义: to turn away (one's eyes or thoughts) or prevent (something harmful) from happening
例句:
- The government took emergency measures to avert an economic crisis.
- She averted her gaze when he asked the uncomfortable question.
释义_raw: vt. 转开, 避免, 防止

释义: ==n. 航空, 航空学, 飞行术\n[法] 航空, 飞行, 飞行术==
英文释义: the design, development, operation, and use of aircraft, especially heavier-than-air craft
例句:
- The rapid advancement of aviation technology has revolutionized global transportation.
- She pursued a degree in aerospace engineering to contribute to the future of sustainable aviation.
释义_raw: n. 航空, 航空学, 飞行术\n[法] 航空, 飞行, 飞行术

释义: ==vt. 避免, 防止, 撤消\n[法] 避免, 回避, 躲开==
英文释义: v. stay clear from; keep away from; keep out of the way of someone or something\nv. refrain from doing something
搭配: avoid doing
例句:
- Try to avoid making mistakes.
- She avoided looking at him.
释义_raw: vt. 避免, 防止, 撤消\n[法] 避免, 回避, 躲开

释义: ==vt. 等候\nvi. 等待着==
英文释义: to wait for something or someone, especially formally or officially
例句:
- The committee's decision is still awaiting final approval from the board.
- Her groundbreaking research findings are now awaiting publication in a top-tier journal.
释义_raw: vt. 等候\nvi. 等待着

释义: ==a. 醒着的\nvt. 唤醒, 唤起, 使意识到\nvi. 醒来, 被唤起, 意识到==
英文释义: not sleeping; to rouse from sleep or inactivity; to become aware or conscious of something
例句:
- She remained awake long after the others had fallen asleep, reflecting on the day's events.
- The documentary awakened public concern about climate change and spurred widespread activism.
释义_raw: a. 醒着的\nvt. 唤醒, 唤起, 使意识到\nvi. 醒来, 被唤起, 意识到

释义: ==n. 奖品, 裁定, 判决\nvt. 授予, 给予==
英文释义: something given to recognize achievement or as a result of a legal decision
例句:
- The Nobel Prize is one of the most prestigious awards in the world.
- The court awarded damages of $2 million to the plaintiff after finding the defendant liable for negligence.
释义_raw: n. 奖品, 裁定, 判决\nvt. 授予, 给予

释义: ==a. 知道的, 有觉悟的==
英文释义: a. (sometimes followed by `of') having or showing knowledge or understanding or realization or perception
词根词缀: a(在)+ware(注意)→在注意中→意识到的
搭配: aware of
例句:
- Are you aware of the risks?
- She was aware of his presence.
释义_raw: a. 知道的, 有觉悟的

释义: ==a. 可怕的, 庄严的, 虔敬的==
英文释义: s. causing fear or dread or terror
词根词缀: awe(敬畏)+ful→充满敬畏的→可怕的
例句:
- The weather was awful.
- He felt awful about the mistake.
释义_raw: a. 可怕的, 庄严的, 虔敬的

释义: ==a. 笨拙的, 棘手的==
英文释义: causing difficulty, embarrassment, or inconvenience; lacking grace or skill in movement or social interaction
词根词缀: awk(相反)+ward(方向)→方向相反的→笨拙的
例句:
- The sudden silence after his joke made the situation incredibly awkward.
- Negotiating the contract terms proved awkward due to conflicting interests between the two parties.
释义_raw: a. 笨拙的, 棘手的

释义: ==n. 轴\n[计] 坐标轴==

vi. 成串, 丛生
vt. 使聚集
[计] 簇
英文释义: A group of similar things or people gathered closely together.
例句:
- The data points formed a tight cluster around the mean, indicating low variability.
- Urban planners are concerned about the rapid cluster of high-rise buildings in the city center, which strains infrastructure.
释义_raw:
n. 串, 丛, 群, 簇
vi. 成串, 丛生
vt. 使聚集
[计] 簇

释义:

vt. 抓住, 踩汽车离合器踏板
vi. 抓
[计] 联轴器; 离合器
英文释义: to grasp or hold something tightly; a mechanical device that connects and disconnects rotating shafts, especially in vehicles
例句:
- She clutched her passport tightly as she entered the immigration checkpoint.
- To shift gears smoothly, the driver must depress the clutch pedal fully before engaging the next gear.
释义_raw:
n. 抓紧, 掌握, 离合器, 一窝小鸡
vt. 抓住, 踩汽车离合器踏板
vi. 抓
[计] 联轴器; 离合器

释义: n. 四轮大马车, 教练\nvt. 训练, 指导\nvi. 坐马车旅行, 作指导
英文释义: a person who trains and directs athletes or teams; also, to train or instruct someone in a skill or subject
例句:
- The basketball coach devised a rigorous training regimen to prepare the team for the national championship.
- She hired a writing coach to help her refine her academic essays for graduate school applications.
词根词缀: coach(马车)→教练(原指马车载人)
释义_raw: n. 四轮大马车, 教练\nvt. 训练, 指导\nvi. 坐马车旅行, 作指导

释义: n. 煤, 木炭\nv. 加煤
英文释义: A black or brownish-black solid fossil fuel formed from plant remains, used for energy production; also, to add coal to a fire or furnace.
例句:
- The power plant relies heavily on coal to generate electricity.
- Workers had to coal the steam engine before the long journey across the mountains.
释义_raw: n. 煤, 木炭\nv. 加煤

释义: n. 结合体, 结合, 联合\n[经] 联合, 联盟
英文释义: a temporary alliance or partnership of distinct groups, parties, or nations for a common purpose
例句:
- The two opposition parties formed a coalition to challenge the ruling government in the upcoming election.
- International efforts to address climate change require a broad coalition of governments, NGOs, and private sector actors.
词根词缀: co(共同)+al(生长)+ition(名词)→共同生长→联盟
释义_raw: n. 结合体, 结合, 联合\n[经] 联合, 联盟

释义: a. 粗糙的, 下等的, 粗俗的\n[计] 粗略的
英文释义: rough in texture, low in quality, or lacking in refinement and sensitivity
例句:
- The coarse sandpaper quickly removed the old paint but left deep scratches.
- His coarse humor offended several colleagues during the formal meeting.
释义_raw: a. 粗糙的, 下等的, 粗俗的\n[计] 粗略的

释义: n. 外套\nvt. 外面覆盖, 给...穿外套
英文释义: A piece of outer clothing worn on the upper body for warmth or protection; to cover a surface with a layer of something.
例句:
- She wore a tailored wool coat to the academic conference.
- The researchers coated the nanoparticles with a polymer layer to enhance biocompatibility.
释义_raw: n. 外套\nvt. 外面覆盖, 给...穿外套

释义: n. 可卡因, 古柯碱\n[化] 可卡因; 古柯碱
英文释义: A powerful and highly addictive stimulant drug derived from the leaves of the coca plant, often abused for its euphoric effects.
例句:
- The use of cocaine is strictly prohibited under international drug control treaties.
- Neuroimaging studies have revealed significant structural changes in the brains of chronic cocaine users.
词根词缀: coca(古柯)+ine(化学物质)→古柯碱→可卡因
释义_raw: n. 可卡因, 古柯碱\n[化] 可卡因; 古柯碱

释义: n. 代码, 密码, 法规, 法典\nvt. 把...编码\n[计] 代码
英文释义: A system of symbols, letters, or numbers used to represent information, especially for secrecy or computer processing; also, a set of rules or laws.
例句:
- The software developer wrote clean, well-documented code to ensure maintainability.
- The judge ruled that the new environmental regulation was part of the state's administrative code.
释义_raw: n. 代码, 密码, 法规, 法典\nvt. 把...编码\n[计] 代码

释义: a. 认知的, 认识的
英文释义: relating to the mental processes of perception, memory, judgment, and reasoning
例句:
- Cognitive psychology studies how people acquire, process, and store information.
- The new teaching method aims to enhance students' cognitive flexibility and critical thinking skills.
释义_raw: a. 认知的, 认识的

释义: 连贯的，一致的；条理清楚的
英文释义: (of an argument or theory) logical and consistent; able to speak clearly and logically
词根词缀: co(共同)+her(粘)+ent→粘在一起的→连贯的
搭配: coherent argument, coherent policy, coherent strategy
例句:
- She gave a coherent explanation.
- The essay lacks a coherent structure.
释义_raw: 连贯的，一致的；条理清楚的

释义: a. 黏合在一起的\n[医] 内聚的
英文释义: forming a united whole; sticking together firmly
例句:
- The team demonstrated a cohesive strategy that led to their decisive victory in the debate competition.
- A cohesive essay presents clear logical connections between paragraphs and maintains consistent tone and focus throughout.
词根词缀: co(共同)+hes(粘)+ive(形容词)→粘在一起的→有凝聚力的
释义_raw: a. 黏合在一起的\n[医] 内聚的

释义: n. 线圈, 盘绕, 骚动, 卷\nvt. 盘绕, 卷
英文释义: A length of wire or rope wound into a series of loops, or the act of winding something into such a shape.
例句:
- The electrician carefully connected the two ends of the coil to complete the circuit.
- She watched as the snake began to coil itself around the branch, ready to strike.
释义_raw: n. 线圈, 盘绕, 骚动, 卷\nvt. 盘绕, 卷

释义: vi. 一致, 符合\n[化] 重合
英文释义: to occur at the same time or in the same place; to agree exactly
例句:
- The conference coincides with the annual science fair, making it difficult for many researchers to attend both.
- Her political views closely coincide with those of the new policy initiative.
词根词缀: co(共同)+in(进入)+cid(落下)+e→共同落下→巧合
释义_raw: vi. 一致, 符合\n[化] 重合

释义: n. 可口可乐, 焦炭\nv. (使)成焦炭
英文释义: 1. n. a carbonaceous residue obtained by heating coal in the absence of air; 2. n. a trademarked cola-flavored soft drink; 3. v. to convert (coal or other organic material) into coke by destructive distillation
例句:
- The steel industry relies heavily on coke as a fuel and reducing agent in blast furnaces.
- He ordered a coke with ice, but the waiter mistakenly brought him a glass of water instead.
词根词缀: coke(焦炭)→焦炭；可口可乐简称
释义_raw: n. 可口可乐, 焦炭\nv. (使)成焦炭

释义: vi. 合作, 勾结\n[法] 合作, 合著, 通敌
英文释义: to work together with others, especially on a project or activity
例句:
- Researchers from five universities collaborated to develop the new AI model.
- The two companies collaborated on a joint venture to expand into emerging markets.
词根词缀: col(共同)+labor(劳动)+ate(动词)→共同劳动→合作
释义_raw: vi. 合作, 勾结\n[法] 合作, 合著, 通敌

释义:

vt. 使倒塌, 折叠
[计] 折叠
英文释义: to fall down or give way suddenly; to break down completely, either physically, mentally, economically, or structurally

- After running the marathon without proper hydration, she suffered complete physical collapse.

释义_raw:
n. 崩溃, 倒塌, 虚脱
vi. 倒塌, 崩溃, 瓦解
vt. 使倒塌, 折叠
[计] 折叠

释义: n. 同事, 同僚
英文释义: a person with whom one works in the same profession or organization
例句:
- She collaborated closely with her colleagues on the interdisciplinary research project.
- He consulted several senior colleagues before finalizing the policy proposal.

释义: v. 收集, 聚集, 集中, 搜集\na. 由收到者付款的\nadv. 由收到者付款地
英文释义: to gather or bring together items, information, or people; to receive payment from the recipient (in telephony/post).

- Researchers collected data from over 10,000 participants across five countries.
- The phone call was made collect, meaning the person receiving the call would be charged.

释义_raw: v. 收集, 聚集, 集中, 搜集\na. 由收到者付款的\nadv. 由收到者付款地

英文释义: the act of gathering things together or the group of things gathered
例句:
- The museum's collection of ancient manuscripts is one of the most comprehensive in Europe.
- Researchers emphasized the importance of systematic sample collection to ensure data validity in clinical trials.

释义: a. 集体的, 聚集的, 共同的\n[医] 集体的, 集合的
英文释义: Shared by, done by, or involving a group of people rather than individuals
例句:
- The success of the project depended on collective effort and mutual trust.
- Climate change is a global challenge that demands collective action from all nations.
词根词缀: col(共同)+lect(收集)+ive(形容词)→集体的
释义_raw: a. 集体的, 聚集的, 共同的\n[医] 集体的, 集合的

释义: n. 学院, 大学, 学会
英文释义: An institution of higher education that offers undergraduate degrees, often part of a larger university or an independent institution.
例句:
- She was accepted to Harvard College, where she majored in economics.
- After graduating from college, he pursued a master's degree in computer science at a top-tier university.

英文释义: to crash into something or someone, especially violently; to come into conflict or opposition

- The two cars collided at the intersection due to heavy fog.
- Her pragmatic approach to policy often collides with his idealistic principles.

释义: n. 碰撞, 冲突, 抵触\n[计] 冲突
英文释义: A violent encounter between two or more moving objects, or a clash of ideas, interests, or systems.
例句:
- The collision between the two cars resulted in serious injuries and a major traffic jam.
- There is an inevitable collision between economic growth and environmental sustainability in many developing countries.
词根词缀: col(共同)+lis(碰撞)+ion(名词)→碰撞→冲突

释义: n. 陆军上校, 长官
英文释义: a senior military officer rank, typically above lieutenant colonel and below brigadier general
例句:
- The colonel briefed the joint task force on the new counterinsurgency strategy.
- She was promoted to colonel after two decades of distinguished service in the U.S. Army.

释义: a. 殖民的, 殖民地的\n[法] 殖民地居民
英文释义: relating to colonies or colonialism, especially the political and cultural influence of a foreign power over a dependent territory
例句:
- The colonial architecture in Goa reflects centuries of Portuguese rule.
- Postcolonial studies critically examine the lasting social and economic impacts of colonial administration.

释义: n. 殖民地, 移民队\n[医] 菌(集)落, 菌丛; 移民区
英文释义: A territory under the political control of a distant country; a group of organisms (e.g., bacteria) growing together as a distinct mass; or a settlement of people living abroad who maintain ties with their native land.
例句:
- The American colonies declared independence from Britain in 1776.
- Under the microscope, the bacterial colony appeared as a dense, circular patch on the agar plate.

释义: n. 专栏, 圆柱, 纵队, 列, 柱形物\n[计] 列, 柱形图
英文释义: A vertical arrangement of items, such as text in a newspaper, data in a table, or a solid cylindrical structure.
例句:
- The editor invited her to write a monthly opinion column for the national newspaper.
- In the dataset, the 'revenue' column contains figures for each fiscal quarter.
释义_raw: n. 专栏, 圆柱, 纵队, 列, 柱形物\n[计] 列, 柱形图

释义: n. 头梳, 鸡冠\nvt. 梳头发, 梳毛\nvi. (浪)涌起
英文释义: A toothed tool for arranging or cleaning hair or fur; also, the fleshy red crest on a rooster's head; to pass a comb through (hair or wool) to clean or arrange it; (of waves) to rise and break in a steady succession.
例句:
- She used a fine-toothed comb to remove the tangles from her wet hair.
- The coastal research team observed how swells would comb steadily toward the shore under consistent offshore winds.

释义: n. 争斗, 战斗\nvi. 战斗, 争斗\nvt. 与...战斗, 与...斗争
英文释义: to fight against something harmful or undesirable; a physical struggle or armed conflict
例句:
- The government has launched a nationwide campaign to combat climate change.
- Soldiers were trained to combat enemy forces in urban environments.
释义_raw: n. 争斗, 战斗\nvi. 战斗, 争斗\nvt. 与...战斗, 与...斗争

释义: n. 组合, 合并, 联合\n[计] 组合图
英文释义: A mixture or union of two or more different things, or a set of items selected from a larger group without regard to order.
例句:
- The security system requires a specific combination of numbers to unlock the door.

释义: v. (使)联合, (使)结合\nn. (企业的)联合, 联合收割机
英文释义: to join or unite two or more things or groups into a single entity; or a large agricultural machine that reaps, threshes, and cleans grain in one operation
例句:
- The two tech companies decided to combine their R&D departments to accelerate innovation.
- Modern farms rely on the combine to harvest wheat efficiently during the short growing season.
词根词缀: com(共同)+bin(两个)+e→两个结合→结合

释义: n. 喜剧, 有趣的事情
英文释义: A dramatic work intended to be humorous and typically ending happily.

- The film blends satire and romance, challenging the conventional boundaries of comedy.

释义_raw: n. 喜剧, 有趣的事情

英文释义: A celestial object consisting of ice, dust, and gas that orbits the sun and develops a visible coma and sometimes a tail when near the sun.
例句:
- The Hale-Bopp comet was visible to the naked eye for over 18 months, making it one of the most observed comets in history.
- Astronomers predict that the next bright comet may appear in the night sky within the next decade, offering a rare opportunity for both research and public observation.
释义_raw: n. 彗星

英文释义: n. a professional entertainer who tells jokes and performs humorous routines; also, a magazine or book containing comic strips. adj. causing laughter; humorous or absurd.
例句:
- The stand-up comic delivered a sharp, politically astute routine that left the audience both laughing and reflecting.
- Her dry wit and impeccable timing made her one of the most acclaimed comics in contemporary British theatre.

释义: vt. 纪念

释义: adv. 所以
英文释义: as a result or effect
例句:
- The company failed to meet regulatory standards; consequently, it was fined heavily.
- She had not prepared for the exam; consequently, her performance was poor.

英文释义: tending to resist change and adhere to traditional values, practices, or beliefs; preserving existing conditions or substances
例句:

释义: n. 外部, 表面, 外貌\na. 外部的, 外在的, 表面的
英文释义: the outer surface or outside part of something; outward appearance

释义: n. 外部, 外面\na. 外部的, 客观的, 表面的
英文释义: located or happening outside something; not part of the internal structure or nature
例句:
- The company's financial difficulties were exacerbated by external economic pressures.
- She attributed her failure to external factors rather than personal shortcomings.

释义_raw: n. 外部, 外面\na. 外部的, 客观的, 表面的

释义: a. 熄灭的, 灭绝的, 耗尽的\n[法] 消灭了的, 过时的, 无效的
英文释义: no longer existing or living; having died out or ceased to be used

释义: vt. 熄灭, 使消亡, 压制, 使减色\n[医] 消灭
英文释义: to put out a fire or light; to bring to an end or destroy completely

释义: n. 额外的事物, 另外的收费\na. 额外的, 特别的\nadv. 额外地, 特别地, 非常地

释义: a. 奢侈的, 挥霍无度的, 浪费的

释义: n. 极端, 末端\na. 极端的, 尽头的, 极度的, 偏激的

释义: a. 传说的, 寓言般的, 难以置信的

英文释义: A point where two or more things meet or connect, especially roads, railways, or biological structures.

释义: a. 锋利的, 敏锐的, 强烈的, 敏捷的, 热心的, 渴望的\nn. 挽歌, 痛哭\nv. 唱挽歌, 痛哭

释义:

- She has more experience than any other candidate, making her the strongest choice for the leadership role.

adv. 多, 更多, 进一步

释义: adv. 而且, 此外
英文释义: in addition; besides; used to introduce a further point that supports or strengthens the previous statement
例句:
- The candidate has extensive experience in international trade; moreover, she holds a PhD in economics.
- Climate change poses serious threats to biodiversity; moreover, its socioeconomic impacts are increasingly evident in developing countries.

释义: n. 生物, 人类\na. 不免一死的, 人类的, 临终的

例句:
- After saving for five years, she finally secured a mortgage to purchase her first apartment in London.

例句:
- The primary objective of this research is to investigate the impact of climate change on biodiversity.
- To ensure fairness, the evaluation must be based on objective criteria rather than personal opinions.
词根词缀: ob(反对)+ject(投掷)+ive(形容词)→客观的
释义_raw: n. 目的, 目标, 宗旨, 宾格, 实物\na. 客观的, 如实的, 无偏见的, 宾格的

释义: n. 义务, 责任, 约束, 契约, 恩惠, 债务\n[经] 待付款, 债务, 义务
英文释义: A moral or legal duty to do something, or a binding agreement that requires payment or performance.
例句:
- Fulfilling contractual obligations is essential for maintaining business credibility.
- As a citizen, she felt a strong moral obligation to vote in the national election.
释义_raw: n. 义务, 责任, 约束, 契约, 恩惠, 债务\n[经] 待付款, 债务, 义务

释义: vt. 强制, 施恩惠于, 使感激\nvi. 施恩惠, 帮忙
英文释义: to compel by law, duty, or circumstances; to do a favor for someone
例句:
- The contract obliges both parties to maintain confidentiality.
- She felt obliged to help her colleague finish the report before the deadline.
词根词缀: ob(加强)+lig(捆绑)+e→捆绑在一起→迫使
释义_raw: vt. 强制, 施恩惠于, 使感激\nvi. 施恩惠, 帮忙

释义: a. 模糊的, 昏暗的, 难解的, 晦涩的, 偏僻的, 微贱的, 无名的\nvt. 使变暗, 隐藏, 使变模糊, 使难解
英文释义: not clear, easy to understand, or see; not famous or important
例句:
- The author's argument is deliberately obscure, making it difficult for readers to grasp the central thesis.
- Her early poems remained obscure until a major literary journal republished them decades later.
词根词缀: ob(加强)+scur(覆盖)+e→完全覆盖→模糊的
释义_raw: a. 模糊的, 昏暗的, 难解的, 晦涩的, 偏僻的, 微贱的, 无名的\nvt. 使变暗, 隐藏, 使变模糊, 使难解

释义: n. 观察, 注意, 观测, 观察力\n[化] 观察
英文释义: the process of watching and recording something carefully, especially to learn more about it
例句:
- Her keen observation of patient behavior led to an early diagnosis.
- The experiment requires precise observation and accurate data recording.
词根词缀: ob(加强)+serv(服务)+ation(名词)→观察行为→观察
释义_raw: n. 观察, 注意, 观测, 观察力\n[化] 观察

释义: vt. 觉察到, 遵守, 注意到, 庆祝\nvi. 注意, 评论
英文释义: to notice or perceive something; to comply with a rule, law, or custom; to celebrate a formal occasion
例句:
- Scientists observed a significant decrease in carbon emissions after the policy was implemented.
- All employees are required to observe the company's code of conduct strictly.
释义_raw: vt. 觉察到, 遵守, 注意到, 庆祝\nvi. 注意, 评论

释义: n. 困扰, 困扰人的情绪\n[医] 强迫观念
英文释义: an idea or desire that constantly occupies a person's mind, often causing anxiety or distress
例句:
- Her obsession with perfection led to chronic stress and burnout.
- The novel explores the protagonist's dangerous obsession with revenge, blurring the line between justice and pathology.
词根词缀: ob(加强)+sess(坐)+ion(名词)→坐立不安→痴迷
释义_raw: n. 困扰, 困扰人的情绪\n[医] 强迫观念

释义: a. 荒废的, 成废物的, 陈旧的, 老式的\nn. 废词, 废物
英文释义: no longer in use or no longer useful, especially because something newer or better has been invented
例句:
- Many typewriters have become obsolete since the widespread adoption of personal computers.
- The term 'horseless carriage' is now obsolete and rarely appears in modern technical writing.
词根词缀: ob(离开)+sol(习惯)+ete→不再习惯→过时的
释义_raw: a. 荒废的, 成废物的, 陈旧的, 老式的\nn. 废词, 废物

释义: n. 障碍, 妨害物, 阻碍
英文释义: Something that blocks or hinders progress or achievement
例句:
- Lack of funding remains a major obstacle to the project's completion.
- She overcame numerous personal and academic obstacles to earn her PhD.
词根词缀: ob(在对面)+sta(站立)+cle(小)→站在对面的东西→障碍
释义_raw: n. 障碍, 妨害物, 阻碍

释义: vt. 阻隔, 妨碍, 阻塞, 遮没\nvi. 设障碍
英文释义: to block or hinder something from happening, moving, or being seen
例句:
- The fallen tree obstructed the road and delayed emergency vehicles.
- His refusal to cooperate seriously obstructed the investigation into the financial fraud.
词根词缀: ob(在对面)+struct(建造)→在对面建造→阻碍
释义_raw: vt. 阻隔, 妨碍, 阻塞, 遮没\nvi. 设障碍

释义: n. 障碍, 妨碍, 闭塞物\n[医] 梗阻, 不通
英文释义: Something that blocks or hinders movement, progress, or function; a physical or abstract barrier.
例句:
- The fallen tree caused a serious obstruction on the highway, leading to a two-hour traffic delay.
- In medical imaging, intestinal obstruction is often identified by the presence of dilated bowel loops and air-fluid levels.
释义_raw: n. 障碍, 妨碍, 闭塞物\n[医] 梗阻, 不通

释义: vt. 获得, 达到\nvi. 流行, 得到公认
英文释义: to get or acquire something, often with effort or formally
例句:
- She obtained a scholarship after months of rigorous preparation.
- The new policy has not yet obtained widespread support among senior faculty members.
释义_raw: vt. 获得, 达到\nvi. 流行, 得到公认

释义: n. 场合, 时机, 机会, 诱因, 理由\nvt. 惹起, 引起
英文释义: a particular time when something happens; a special or formal event; a reason or cause for something
例句:
- The wedding was a joyous occasion that brought together family members from across the country.
- His careless remarks on social media occasioned widespread criticism and calls for accountability.
